task_completion.description: Modify the shared-infra docker-compose.yml to attach PostgreSQL and Redis to their respective external networks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/shared-infra/docker-compose.yml. The postgres service should have a 'networks' section including 'infra-db-net', and the redis service should include 'infra-cache-net'. A top-level 'networks' block should declare both as external.

task_completion.description: Modify the flask-api docker-compose.yml to attach the API service to both external networks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/flask-api/docker-compose.yml. The api service should have a 'networks' section including both 'infra-db-net' and 'infra-cache-net'. A top-level 'networks' block should declare both as external.

task_completion.description: Start the Docker Compose services for both projects.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'docker compose up' (or 'docker-compose up') being executed successfully for both projects, or a 'docker ps' command showing 'shared-postgres', 'shared-redis', and 'flask-api' containers running.

task_completion.description: Verify database connectivity via the Flask app's DB health check endpoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser or terminal (using curl/wget) showing a successful HTTP response from http://localhost:5050/health/db indicating the database is connected/healthy.

task_completion.description: Verify Redis connectivity via the Flask app's Redis health check endpoint.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser or terminal (using curl/wget) showing a successful HTTP response from http://localhost:5050/health/redis indicating Redis is connected/healthy.

task_completion.description: Save a report confirming the network setup and connection status.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the creation and content of a file named 'connectivity-report.txt' in /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/. The content must mention the network setup and the successful connection status.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent actually executed Docker commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal screenshots should show standard Docker daemon output (e.g., pulling images, creating networks, starting containers) rather than mocked or echoed text.

integrity.description: Verify that the health checks were performed via actual HTTP requests.
integrity.visual_evidence: The health checks must be performed using a real HTTP client (like a web browser, curl, or wget) targeting localhost:5050, rather than just opening a local file or echoing a string.

integrity.description: Verify that the connectivity report was actually saved to the disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report file should be visibly saved to the correct path (e.g., shown in a file explorer, terminal 'ls' output, or a text editor title bar without an 'unsaved' indicator).

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project root: /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects. External networks: infra-db-net, infra-cache-net. Services: shared-postgres, shared-redis, flask-api. API Port: 5050. Expected report path: /home/ga/Documents/docker-projects/connectivity-report.txt.
